events.title: Children and Beginners
events.excerpt: It is a great introduction to Karate, and training up to 5th Kyu (Yellow Belt). Grades and Adults are welcome in the beginners session. New to Karate? Wondering what to wear? Read the Frequently Asked Questions for more information.
description: A great introduction to Karate, and training up to 5th Kyu (Yellow Belt).
Grades and Adults are welcome in the beginners session.
New to Karate? Wondering what to wear? Read the Frequently Asked Questions for more information.
Training for the upcoming Grading will focus on:
White – Kata Heian Shodan and Heian Nidan

events.title: Grades and Adults
events.excerpt: Training for those at 9th Kyu (Yellow Belt) and above. This session could include any aspect of Karate (see our training materials to see the different karate techniques and applications). As we approach our bi-annual grading events you will find classes focused on preparing those looking to take part. Bring a water bottle to rehydrate during the mid-session break. Those below 9th Kyu (Yellow Belt) wishing to join this session should talk to an instructor. See Our Ranks for more information on belt order.
description: Training for those at 5th Kyu (Yellow Belt) and above.
This session could include any aspect of Karate (see the training materials to see the different karate techniques and applications).
Bring a water bottle to rehydrate during the mid-session break.
Those below 5th Kyu (Yellow Belt) wishing to join this session should talk to an instructor.
See Our Ranks for more information on belt order.
Training for the upcoming Grading will focus on:
Yellow – Kata Heian Sandan and Heian Yondan
Orange – Kata Heian Yondan and Heian Godan
Green – Kata Tekki Shodan and Jitte
Blue – Kata Tekki Nidan and Empi
Brown – Kata Tekki Nidan and Bassai Dai

events.title: Grading
events.excerpt: The summer grading event where students can register to be assessed to demonstrate their karate skills, techniques and knowledge. Successful students will be presented their new rank in a presentation at the end of the event. As we near the event, students will need to register to attend this event so we can prepare for the number attending. Please talk to any of the black belt team or Karen Payne at a training session. Those attending the event for assessment must pay the grading fee in advance. All graded students must keep an up to date karate license, which must be in place ahead of the event. New licenses, and license renewals, must be paid for ahead of the event. You can read more about our ranks, grading rules and how grading works in more detail by following the links in the header.
description: The summer grading event where students can register to be assessed to demonstrate their karate skills, techniques and knowledge. Successful students will be presented their new rank in a presentation at the end of the event.
As we near the event, students will need to register to attend this event so we can prepare for the number attending. Please talk to any of the black belt team or Karen Payne at a training session.
Those attending the event for assessment must pay the grading fee in advance.
All graded students must keep an up to date karate license, which must be in place ahead of the event. New licenses, and license renewals, must be paid for ahead of the event.
You can read more about our ranks , grading rules , and how grading works . The video below is a great way to see what to expect on the day!
Things to remember about the grading event:

The grading starts promptly at 1pm and ends when the final presentation is made. It is expected that students attend the entire event from start to finish. Leaving early may result in your grade not being awarded.
Students are encouraged to attend early to stretch and run through any final preparation. The training hall will be open and available to students from 12pm.
When taking a grading it is important to present well, with a clean and pressed gi, clean hands and feet and trimmed nails.
When the grading is finished and presentations have been complete, the bar will open and a buffet will be available for all those taking part.
